meaning: remembrance/mention (as the subject of this verse)
Zakariya (a proper name meaning 'God has remembered')
and it became intensely lit up
I feared (referring to Prophet Zakariya expressing his concern)
the protectors (who Zakariya feared would mishandle inheritance after him)
so grant! (to Zakariya asking Allah for a child)
Jacob (as the object being inherited from)
very pleasing (as a quality of something/someone)
we make (referring to Allah in this ayah)
and she was (connecting to previous statement about having a son)
your sign (referring to the miracle given to Zechariah)
O Yahya! (calling out to Prophet Yahya)
and tender compassion (as a divine gift)
and then she took for herself
righteous, pious (describing someone in the ayah)
so that I may give as a gift
and so that we make him
so she became pregnant with him (referring to Jesus)
to a place (that she withdrew to)
and then labor pains came to her
Oh, if only I (said by Maryam expressing regret)
and then he called to her
fresh ripe dates (that will fall upon you)
then if ever (connecting to previous statement)
so/then never (will I speak to anyone today)
she carries him (referring to baby Jesus)
Aaron (being addressed as 'O sister of Aaron')
to him (the one they're pointing to)
who (asking about the person they will speak to)
the cradle (that baby Jesus was in)
and very good and kind (to my mother)
who/which/that (connects 'Isa son of Mary' to what follows)
that (connects 'it was not for Allah' to 'He should take')
Allah (the one true God who is being worshipped)
and so they differed (from each other)
on the day (when they come to us)
they believe/trust (in this ayah: they do not believe)
and to us (they will return to us)
when (referring to past time when he said to his father)
what (that did not come to you)
upon you (addressing Abraham in this ayah)
he was (past tense, describing how Allah was with him)
and I withdraw myself from you all
and we called him out loudly
and mention! (connecting to what comes after: 'in the Book, Ismail')
a prophet (as the object being described)
Adam (as the object being talked about)
Abraham (mentioned in list with other prophets)
they fell down prostrate suddenly/forcefully (when hearing Allah's verses)
and weeping (describing their state during prostration)
after them (referring to the previous people mentioned)
they meet/encounter (referring to those who wasted prayer)
except (introduces an exception to the previous statement)
anything/at all (that will not be wronged)
The Most Merciful (one of Allah's names)
they hear/listen (referring to the people in Paradise)
except (what comes after from what was mentioned before)
and for them (referring to the people of Paradise)
that (pointing to something far away, feminine)
a namesake/equal (in status to someone else)
we will certainly make them present (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
kneeling intensely (as the state they were brought in)
with/by her/it (referring to Hell/Fire mentioned earlier)
burning intensely (as a state or condition)
and if (connects to previous statement while introducing the condition 'any of you')
decreed (as a matter that is settled)
we rescue (those who were mindful of Allah)
fallen on their knees (describing their state in Hell)
they believed (those who disbelieved said to those who believed)
a gathering place/assembly (as object being described)
better/more beautiful (than others mentioned in the ayah)
and he increases (Allah increases the guidance of those who seek guidance)
as a reward (specifying how something is better)
I will surely be given (with strong emphasis)
No! Never! (strongly rejecting what was said)
and we extend (to him from punishment)
strength/power (as the object being sought in this ayah)
No! By no means! (strong rejection of what was mentioned before)
upon them, against them (referring to the false gods they worship)
the ones who reject truth (that the devils are sent against)
and we drive forward (the criminals to Hell)
as a thirsty group (being driven to Hell like cattle to water)
except (those who have permission to intercede)
because of it (referring to their claim about Allah)
that (connects 'it is not befitting for the Most Merciful' to 'take a son')
the resurrection (on the Day of Judgment)
those who - connects descriptions to people or things
so that you give good news
for/to them (masculine plural, referring to the destroyed generations)
